name: Developer Product Minimal
firstViewport: Sharp headline, terse product explanation, command/code or product preview, direct docs/signup action.
layoutRules: Prioritize product proof over decoration.
Use code-native UI, docs links, and crisp grid alignment.
typography: Tight, highly legible, technical, with controlled heading scale.
risks: Can feel generic if the product preview is weak.

name: Operational SaaS Console
avoidFor: Consumer launches that need emotional storytelling
firstViewport: Product UI is the hero: sidebar, rows, status, selected state, and clear primary action.
layoutRules: Favor app shell, compact rows, and real states.
Do not turn dense workflows into card-only marketing sections.
typography: Product UI typography with compact labels and strong information hierarchy.
risks: May feel too restrained if the brief expects a big launch moment.

name: Enterprise Trust Solution
avoidFor: Playful consumer products or experimental portfolios
firstViewport: Clear value proposition, trust proof, architecture or workflow diagram, sales CTA.
layoutRules: Use proof, governance, and flow diagrams.
Keep visuals polished but conservative.
typography: Executive-readable hierarchy with measured display type and clean body copy.
risks: Can become bland if no specific proof points are included.

name: Data Dashboard Command Center
firstViewport: Dense but ordered shell with metric summary, chart, table, filters, and selected state.
layoutRules: Use tables and charts where the workflow needs them.
Make density scannable, not decorative.
typography: Compact UI typography with strong numeric emphasis.
risks: Easy to overfill with fake metrics; seed data must feel domain-specific.

name: Editorial Technical Docs
firstViewport: Typographic hero, concise explanation, docs entry point, architecture or code sample.
layoutRules: Let typography and information architecture lead.
Use diagrams and examples instead of generic feature cards.
typography: Editorial hierarchy with readable prose and code-friendly monospace accents.
risks: Can undersell commercial value if no product outcome is shown.

name: Motion Rich Launch
firstViewport: Strong visual hook, animated proof or product reveal, short CTA path.
layoutRules: Use motion as hierarchy, not filler.
Keep core copy short and readable.
typography: Display-forward but readable, with restrained UI text.
risks: Can become gimmicky if every section animates.

name: Consumer Brand Story
avoidFor: Dense admin tools and technical documentation
firstViewport: Brand name, emotional promise, image-led product or lifestyle signal, simple CTA.
layoutRules: Use real or generated visual assets.
Avoid dashboard chrome unless the product is software-led.
typography: Expressive heading paired with clear body and CTA text.
risks: Needs strong imagery; otherwise it feels like a generic landing page.

name: Portfolio Studio Grid
firstViewport: Name or studio mark, sharp positioning, large work preview, restrained navigation.
layoutRules: Let work samples carry the page.
Use consistent image ratios and editorial spacing.
typography: Confident editorial type with disciplined captions and metadata.
risks: Can obscure conversion if contact path is too subtle.

name: Commerce Premium Catalog
firstViewport: Product-first hero, price or collection signal, clear purchase or browse path.
layoutRules: Use real product imagery and stable product grids.
Do not hide purchase affordances behind decorative layouts.
typography: Retail-readable hierarchy with crisp product metadata.
risks: Needs product assets; placeholders will break the premium feel.

name: AI Lab Research Notebook
avoidFor: Consumer shopping and simple local business sites
firstViewport: Research statement, benchmark or diagram, model/product artifact, direct technical CTA.
layoutRules: Use figures, tables, code, and benchmark blocks.
Keep experimental visuals readable and grounded.
typography: Research-paper clarity with modern product polish.
risks: Can feel academic if product action is not visible.

name: Fintech Precision Trust
firstViewport: Financial outcome, trust proof, high-quality chart or report preview, controlled CTA.
layoutRules: Use clear numeric hierarchy and cautious color semantics.
Do not use noisy speculative visuals.
typography: Precise numeric typography and conservative headings.
risks: Needs careful chart semantics; fake finance data looks untrustworthy.

name: Learning Platform Friendly
firstViewport: Outcome-oriented headline, product/course preview, friendly progress or lesson state.
layoutRules: Show progress, lessons, outcomes, and guidance.
Avoid childish visuals unless the audience is children.
typography: Warm but structured hierarchy with readable learning content.
risks: Can become too soft for professional training unless density is tuned.

name: Developer CLI Workbench
firstViewport: Documentation shell with a decisive install command, workflow steps, keyboard hints, and a visible terminal result.
layoutRules: Treat commands and output as primary content, not decoration.
Keep navigation compact and make copy actions unmistakable.
typography: Compact sans-serif UI paired with a highly legible monospace command layer.
risks: Dense terminal surfaces can intimidate newcomers without progressive disclosure.

name: Developer API Observability Lab
firstViewport: Operational dashboard with service health, latency trend, error groups, trace filters, and a selected incident.
layoutRules: Anchor the hierarchy on actionable anomalies and time ranges.
Use semantic color only for status and severity.
typography: Dense product typography with tabular numerals and monospace identifiers.
risks: Too many equal-weight metrics can hide the incident that needs attention.

name: Open Source Community Hub
firstViewport: Community-led project grid with an adoption statement, repository proof, contributor paths, and ecosystem examples.
layoutRules: Show real projects, contributors, and extension categories early.
Keep contribution and documentation paths equally visible.
typography: Confident technical headings with friendly metadata and compact repository labels.
risks: Community claims feel hollow without current repository and contributor evidence.

name: Collaborative Workspace Canvas
firstViewport: Live workspace shell with a flexible canvas, participant presence, contextual toolbar, comments, and a template rail.
layoutRules: Let the canvas dominate while keeping tools reachable at the edge.
Use presence and comments to make collaboration tangible.
typography: Neutral workspace typography with concise tool labels and readable object text.
risks: A crowded toolbar can overwhelm the primary collaborative surface.

name: Automation Flow Builder
firstViewport: Node-based workflow proof with trigger, policy, and action stages plus an integration picker and run status.
layoutRules: Make data flow and branching explicit.
Separate configuration, execution status, and error recovery.
typography: Compact operational typography with clear node titles and parameter labels.
risks: Abstract node diagrams need realistic labels to communicate actual value.

name: Customer Success Inbox
firstViewport: Queue-oriented dashboard with workload metrics, prioritized conversations, customer context, and resolution states.
layoutRules: Keep conversation urgency and ownership visible at a glance.
Balance operational density with enough customer context to respond well.
typography: Friendly compact UI text with strong timestamps, ownership, and status labels.
risks: Metric-heavy layouts can make a human support workflow feel mechanical.

name: Enterprise Infrastructure Blueprint
firstViewport: Architecture-led solution brief with system boundaries, deployment proof, reliability figures, and a technical consultation CTA.
layoutRules: Use a real system model instead of generic cloud illustrations.
Connect every technical claim to an operational or business outcome.
typography: Measured technical hierarchy with diagram labels optimized for executive scanning.
risks: Architecture density can obscure the decision narrative for nontechnical buyers.

name: Governance Evidence Room
firstViewport: Document-led evidence room with control domains, current certifications, policy updates, and gated audit materials.
layoutRules: Expose evidence freshness, ownership, and scope.
Organize controls for retrieval rather than marketing drama.
typography: Formal document typography with compact tables, dates, and control identifiers.
risks: Stale dates or vague certification labels will damage credibility.

name: Executive Transformation Brief
firstViewport: Outcome statement followed by a portfolio of initiatives, quantified change, customer proof, and an executive briefing CTA.
layoutRules: Frame the narrative around before, intervention, and measurable after.
Use a small number of strong proof modules instead of feature grids.
typography: Executive editorial type with large outcome statements and disciplined evidence captions.
risks: Broad transformation language becomes generic without quantified outcomes.

name: Incident Response Wallboard
firstViewport: Dark operational wallboard with active incidents, affected services, response timeline, severity filters, and ownership.
layoutRules: Reserve the strongest contrast for active customer impact.
Keep time, owner, and next action visible in every incident row.
typography: High-contrast operational type with tabular time and terse incident labels.
risks: Excessive alarm color creates fatigue and weakens severity distinctions.

name: Executive KPI Briefing
firstViewport: Briefing-style dashboard with outcome headline, target variance, three strategic pillars, and a concise decision queue.
layoutRules: Limit the first view to decision-grade measures.
Pair each variance with context, owner, and recommended action.
typography: Executive-readable headings with prominent numerals and concise analytical notes.
risks: Oversimplified KPIs can conceal operational causes and uncertainty.

name: Field Operations Mapboard
firstViewport: Map-dominant operations shell with regional status, live assignments, route exceptions, and a compact dispatch queue.
layoutRules: Keep spatial state primary and lists synchronized to map selection.
Make exceptions actionable without covering the map.
typography: Compact field-operations UI with readable location codes and timestamps.
risks: Dense overlays can make geographic relationships unreadable.

name: API Reference Workbench
firstViewport: Three-pane reference workbench with endpoint navigation, parameter documentation, runnable request, and response output.
layoutRules: Keep navigation, explanation, and runnable code synchronized.
Make prerequisites and error responses as discoverable as happy paths.
typography: Reading-first sans serif with dense monospace tables and code samples.
risks: A visually polished reference still fails if examples and errors are incomplete.

name: Product Guides Library
firstViewport: Search-led guide library with role-based paths, featured tasks, recently updated content, and clear reading progression.
layoutRules: Organize around user tasks rather than internal feature names.
Show content freshness and estimated effort.
typography: Comfortable prose hierarchy with restrained labels and generous line height.
risks: Too many category cards can recreate the navigation problem instead of solving it.

name: Knowledge Base Help Center
firstViewport: Friendly support path with prominent search, issue categories, guided resolution progress, and escalation context.
layoutRules: Lead with the language customers use when describing problems.
Offer guided steps before exposing the full article archive.
typography: Warm instructional type with clear step labels and highly readable answers.
risks: Friendly visuals cannot compensate for weak search vocabulary or stale answers.

name: Cinematic Product Reveal
firstViewport: Full-bleed reveal moment with one striking artifact, a short promise, launch timing, and a single invitation.
layoutRules: Sequence motion around one product reveal.
Keep copy and interaction sparse enough to preserve cinematic focus.
typography: Large display typography paired with minimal, quiet interface labels.
risks: Large media and motion can harm performance and accessibility if not carefully constrained.

name: Proof Led Early Access
firstViewport: Direct launch claim beside a working product proof, a short beta contract, and immediate access or waitlist action.
layoutRules: Let a believable product interaction carry the launch.
State access conditions, maturity, and expected outcome plainly.
typography: Crisp product typography with monospace proof and compact beta labels.
risks: A weak or noninteractive product proof makes the launch feel premature.

name: Community Countdown Campaign
firstViewport: Bold countdown chapter with a collectible visual, community activity, release milestones, and a share or reminder action.
layoutRules: Turn time and participation into the visual rhythm.
Use social proof and milestones without cluttering the reveal.
typography: Playful display type balanced by clear timing and participation labels.
risks: Novelty styling can date quickly or obscure essential launch details.

name: Wellness Companion Soft
firstViewport: Soft daily ritual story with a reassuring promise, human moment, simple progress cue, and low-pressure signup.
layoutRules: Use calm pacing and concrete daily benefits.
Keep sensitive progress data supportive rather than competitive.
typography: Rounded, warm headings with highly readable supportive copy.
risks: Overly soft language may weaken credibility for evidence-based health products.

name: Media Discovery Playground
firstViewport: Art-led discovery grid with a personalized collection, active media moment, taste signals, and an immediate play action.
layoutRules: Let cover art and collections define the information architecture.
Preserve readable controls over expressive media color.
typography: Bold cultural headings with compact, familiar media controls.
risks: A dense wall of artwork can make hierarchy and accessibility inconsistent.

name: Local Service Concierge
firstViewport: Location-aware service selection with nearby availability, trust signals, clear pricing, and a direct booking path.
layoutRules: Prioritize availability, distance, price, and trust.
Keep the browse-to-book path short and reversible.
typography: Friendly transactional typography with prominent time, distance, and price metadata.
risks: Insufficient provider and availability detail undermines booking confidence.

name: Case Study Narrative
firstViewport: Editorial case opening with problem, role, constraints, a flagship artifact, and a clear impact summary.
layoutRules: Structure each story around decisions and evidence, not a gallery dump.
Keep role, collaborators, timeline, and outcomes explicit.
typography: Editorial long-form hierarchy with disciplined captions and pull metrics.
risks: Long narratives lose credibility when process detail is not tied to outcomes.

name: Experimental Creative Canvas
firstViewport: Asymmetric project canvas with overlapping artifacts, active cursor or motion cues, and a compact identity statement.
layoutRules: Break the grid intentionally while preserving project labels and navigation.
Use interaction to reveal relationships, not to hide content.
typography: Expressive display type with rigorous project metadata and navigation labels.
risks: Experimental interaction can become inaccessible or difficult to navigate.

name: Technical Builder Showcase
firstViewport: Technical identity, selected shipped projects, repository proof, concise writing index, and a direct contact path.
layoutRules: Show what was built, why it matters, and what is live.
Use code and system artifacts selectively as proof.
typography: Tight technical sans serif with monospace project metadata.
risks: Repository counts without project context read as vanity metrics.

name: Marketplace Discovery Grid
firstViewport: Search-and-filter catalog with varied product cards, seller context, collection shortcuts, and visible sort state.
layoutRules: Make filtering reversible and preserve discovery context.
Keep product, price, seller, and delivery signals comparable.
typography: Retail UI typography optimized for scanning names, prices, and seller metadata.
risks: Inconsistent imagery and metadata can make a marketplace feel untrustworthy.

name: Direct Response Product Detail
firstViewport: Large product moment with price, variant selection, benefit proof, fulfillment promise, and persistent purchase action.
layoutRules: Keep selection, price, delivery, and purchase action adjacent.
Sequence proof from tangible benefit to specification and reassurance.
typography: Confident product display type with crisp transactional labels and specifications.
risks: A dramatic product story can push essential buying information too far down the page.

name: Wholesale Order Workbench
firstViewport: Account-aware order table with SKU search, contract pricing, availability, quantity editing, and a persistent order summary.
layoutRules: Optimize for keyboard entry and repeated purchasing.
Keep account terms, stock, and order impact visible while editing.
typography: Dense tabular typography with strong SKU, price, and quantity alignment.
risks: Consumer-style product cards slow down high-volume ordering workflows.

name: Benchmark Evaluation Board
firstViewport: Evaluation dashboard with task filters, ranked results, confidence context, methodology access, and selected model detail.
layoutRules: Expose methodology, uncertainty, and dataset scope beside rankings.
Separate comparable results from marketing claims.
typography: Dense analytical type with tabular numerals, compact labels, and readable footnotes.
risks: Rankings without uncertainty and methodology invite misleading conclusions.

name: Scientific Publication Archive
firstViewport: Editorial publication index with a featured finding, topic navigation, author and date metadata, and citation-ready records.
layoutRules: Optimize for scanning title, abstract, authors, venue, and date.
Use figures to support findings, not as decorative thumbnails.
typography: Scholarly editorial hierarchy with comfortable abstracts and compact citation metadata.
risks: Editorial treatment must not obscure archival completeness or citation details.

name: Experiment Control Workbench
firstViewport: Run-oriented workbench with experiment queue, parameter set, artifact preview, comparison state, and reproducibility metadata.
layoutRules: Keep inputs, versioned context, outputs, and status traceable.
Support side-by-side comparison without hiding failed runs.
typography: Compact technical UI with monospace run identifiers and tabular parameters.
risks: Missing provenance and version context makes results difficult to reproduce.

name: Consumer Money Companion
firstViewport: Friendly money overview with available balance, spending context, a clear next action, and visible progress toward a goal.
layoutRules: Translate financial state into understandable actions.
Use positive guidance without hiding fees, risk, or constraints.
typography: Approachable numeric typography with plain-language guidance and strong accessibility.
risks: Friendly styling must not trivialize risk, fees, or account limitations.

name: Treasury Operations Console
firstViewport: Institutional console with cash position, account movements, forecast variance, approvals, and reconciliation exceptions.
layoutRules: Keep balances, currency, timing, and approval state unambiguous.
Separate expected movement from reconciled fact.
typography: Tabular institutional typography with precise currencies, dates, and approval labels.
risks: Ambiguous currency or settlement state can create operational risk.

name: Investment Research Terminal
firstViewport: Research terminal with market context, thesis note, comparative chart, catalysts, risks, and linked source evidence.
layoutRules: Separate observed data, analyst interpretation, and scenario assumptions.
Keep sources and timestamps adjacent to decision inputs.
typography: Dense analytical typography with tabular market data and readable research prose.
risks: Visual urgency can encourage false confidence when uncertainty is not explicit.

name: Cohort Learning Workspace
firstViewport: Learning workspace with current module, cohort activity, assignment queue, instructor feedback, and visible shared progress.
layoutRules: Connect content, practice, feedback, and peer activity in one workflow.
Keep deadlines and next learning action visible.
typography: Adult learning typography with clear module structure and compact collaboration metadata.
risks: Social activity can distract from the learner's next required action.

name: Kids Exploration Path
firstViewport: Colorful learning world with one clear mission, friendly character or artifact, visible progress, and a separate parent trust cue.
layoutRules: Use large targets, short language, and one task per moment.
Keep reward mechanics supportive and age-appropriate.
typography: Large rounded type for children with plain, calm supporting text for adults.
risks: Overstimulation and manipulative rewards can undermine learning and parent trust.

name: Certification Study Console
firstViewport: Study console with readiness score, domain breakdown, current lesson, practice queue, and evidence for completed competencies.
layoutRules: Tie every progress signal to assessed knowledge or practice evidence.
Keep weak domains and recommended next steps explicit.
typography: Structured professional type with clear scores, domain labels, and readable study content.
risks: Progress percentages can create false confidence without valid assessment evidence.

name: Developer Dashboard Grid Data
avoidFor: Projects that require a different information-density policy.
Interfaces whose primary emphasis conflicts with this direction.
firstViewport: Use an ordered metric and workflow grid with visible operational state. Keep metrics, trends, and decision signals visually dominant.
layoutRules: Use an ordered metric and workflow grid with visible operational state.
Keep metrics, trends, and decision signals visually dominant.
Use compact spacing while preserving scan paths and target separation.
Use restrained motion for continuity, feedback, and state changes only.
typography: Precise technical sans-serif typography with restrained monospace accents.
risks: Compact spacing can reduce clarity when too many secondary states are shown.

name: Launch Centered Hero Story
avoidFor: Projects that require a different information-density policy.
Interfaces whose primary emphasis conflicts with this direction.
firstViewport: Use a centered opening statement followed by one concrete proof surface. Keep narrative progression and memorable visual moments dominant.
layoutRules: Use a centered opening statement followed by one concrete proof surface.
Keep narrative progression and memorable visual moments dominant.
Use spacious grouping and limit each viewport to a small number of focal elements.
Use expressive motion only for hierarchy and product-state transitions.
typography: Expressive display typography constrained by a calm supporting text system.
risks: Spacious composition can hide important comparison context when content grows.
Expressive motion can distract from product evidence when overused.

name: Developer Dashboard Grid Data
avoidFor: Projects that require a different information-density policy.
Interfaces whose primary emphasis conflicts with this direction.
firstViewport: Use an ordered metric and workflow grid with visible operational state. Keep metrics, trends, and decision signals visually dominant.
layoutRules: Use an ordered metric and workflow grid with visible operational state.
Keep metrics, trends, and decision signals visually dominant.
Use balanced spacing with clear grouping and moderate information density.
Use restrained motion for continuity, feedback, and state changes only.
typography: Precise technical sans-serif typography with restrained monospace accents.
risks: Balanced visual treatment still requires domain-specific content and evidence.

name: Consumer Centered Hero Story
avoidFor: Projects that require a different information-density policy.
Interfaces whose primary emphasis conflicts with this direction.
firstViewport: Use a centered opening statement followed by one concrete proof surface. Keep narrative progression and memorable visual moments dominant.
layoutRules: Use a centered opening statement followed by one concrete proof surface.
Keep narrative progression and memorable visual moments dominant.
Use balanced spacing with clear grouping and moderate information density.
Use restrained motion for continuity, feedback, and state changes only.
typography: Humanist sans-serif typography with approachable rhythm and clear labels.
risks: Balanced visual treatment still requires domain-specific content and evidence.
